note: GOLDEN fixture: golden-manifest.json is the VERBATIM raw HTTP body of GET /reels/{name} served by the REAL backend (offline mode), and this file records the manifest_hash it seals. It pins the browser-side canonicalizer (src/lib/provenance-verify.ts) to the backend's canonical hash (src/cinemory/provenance.py). Regenerate from the repo root with: PYTHONPATH=src python -c "from fastapi.testclient import TestClient; import cinemory.api as api, pathlib; c = TestClient(api.app); name = 'golden-μνήμη-reel 🎬 ✨'; r = c.post('/reels', json={'name': name, 'chapters': 2, 'per_chapter': 2, 'occasion': 'wedding'}); fx = pathlib.Path('frontend/src/test/fixtures'); g = c.get(f'/reels/{name}'); fx.joinpath('golden-manifest.json').write_bytes(g.content); print(r.json()['manifest_hash'])" — then paste the printed hash here. The name deliberately exercises non-ASCII + astral chars (ensure_ascii escaping) and the wedding occasion carries integral float lexemes (88.0/4.0).
